era: 1980-1981. Hardcore Punk. E=8 -- earliest Seattle hardcore, self-released, maximum raw energy, pre-infrastructure.
associated_musicians: Paul Solger (also in The Fartz, degree 270)

theory_nodes.concept: Earliest Seattle hardcore: Solger (1980) at or before The Fartz (1980). First wave of American hardcore. Parallel to Black Flag, Dead Kennedys, Minor Threat -- not in response to them.
theory_nodes.description: Being first. The condition of absence-of-documentation as evidence.

theory_nodes.concept: Paul Solger cross-membership: guitar in Solger, bass in The Fartz (degree 270). Adjacent degrees, adjacent historical moments, same musician. Community is the continuity.
theory_nodes.description: The small-scene cross-membership dynamic: more musical ideas than available musicians.

theory_nodes.concept: Independent / self-released: no label, no commercial distribution, punk underground network only. Maximum Rocknroll, mail order, touring, hand-to-hand. The most raw artifact of the scene.
theory_nodes.description: Before the infrastructure existed. The recordings that survive are the rawest documents.

cross_references.relationship: PAUL SOLGER. Adjacent degrees, adjacent moments. Same musician in both bands. The Fartz had Alternative Tentacles; Solger was independent. Solger is earlier and rawer.
Post-punk follows hardcore. The arc modulates from E=8 hardcore (Solger, Fartz) to post-punk (U-Men). Capitol Hill continuity.
Seattle hardcore continuity. Duff McKagan connects Ten Minute Warning to The Fartz to the broader scene Solger initiated.

sps_pair.pairing_note: Complex whistles and clicks. Community-specific communication: Solger communicates within the Seattle hardcore community; the Southern Residents communicate within their three-pod community. Both are specific, both are endangered (Solger's recordings; the SRKW population).
